citation_and_locator: Leung, Wai-Mun. 2008. `Promising Approaches for the Analysis of Sentence-final Particles in Cantonese: the Case of [aa3]`. Asian Social Science 4(5): 74-82. DOI 10.5539/ass.v4n5p74; sections 2.1 and 3
what_it_supports: Shows that A-not-A and wh hosts are independently interrogative, while `aa3` makes several host types more neutral/moderate without replacing their original force.
limit: The author states that the study is not comprehensive; examples combine a roughly three-hour recorded sample with native-speaker intuition and do not establish every host, prosodic, or interactional boundary.

citation_and_locator: Cheung, Wang Nok. 2023. Investigating the semantic/pragmatic function(s) of the Cantonese sentence final particle aa3. HKU MA thesis, handle 10722/335514
what_it_supports: Classifies softening, subjective evaluation, politeness, intolerance, and information emphasis; reports the first two as most frequent; proposes speculative, deductive, and assumptive epistemic subtypes.
limit: The full PDF was access-blocked in this review; the official abstract does not expose exact examples, annotation decisions, host restrictions, prosody, or negative boundaries.

citation_and_locator: Leung, Wai-Mun. 2010. `On the Identity and Uses of Cantonese Sentence-final Particles in the Late 20th Century: The Case of wo (喎) and bo (噃)`. Asian Social Science 6(1): 13-23. DOI 10.5539/ass.v6n1p13; sections 2-4
what_it_supports: From HKUCC and film data, distinguishes `wo3` realization/reminder/hearsay/contrast from principally contrastive, rare standalone, cluster-prone `bo3`; reports a 602:16 frequency ratio and rejects free interchangeability.
limit: The claims target 1990s Hong Kong Cantonese; they do not automatically extend to current, historical, Guangzhou, regional, or speaker-specific systems, and one residual realization `bo3` case remains.

citation_and_locator: Leung, Wai-Mun. 2010. `The Comparison of the Cantonese Sentence Final Particles bo (噃) and wo (喎): From the 1940s to the 1970s`. Asian Culture and History 2(2): 86-98. DOI 10.5539/ach.v2n2p86; conclusion
what_it_supports: Tracks expansion of `wo3` realization/reminder/hearsay/contrast and contraction of `bo3` toward contrast, while recording the historical relationship rather than treating them as synchronically identical.
limit: Scripted or topic-restricted film dialogue is not fully spontaneous, and diachronic trends do not alone define present-day productivity or individual-speaker grammar.
